title: Application Layer Foundation
purpose: Establish the shared FastAPI application-layer foundation for the next backend phase: `/api/v1` router organization, standard API error responses, common response schemas, mutation success and cursor-pagination conventions, and shared exception translation patterns. This spec intentionally does not implement auth, Pay integration, dashboard/billing aggregation, support, Discord flows, background jobs, or database schema changes.

context.summary: The current repo already has database foundations and a completed rewards application slice, but the browser-facing API layer remains narrow and inconsistent with the newer application architecture docs. The app is versioned through `settings.api_v1_prefix` in `app/main.py`, yet routers still live directly under `app/api/routers/`, only rewards endpoints are registered, and router functions translate service exceptions into ad hoc `HTTPException(detail=...)` payloads instead of a shared standard error DTO. The next workstream should create the reusable API/service/repository conventions that later vertical specs can build on without re-solving these basics.
repo_reality_summary: `app/main.py` already mounts a top-level `api_router` at `settings.api_v1_prefix`, so `/api/v1` versioning exists globally without per-router `/v1` prefixes.
`app/api/routers/__init__.py` currently registers only `reward_notifications`, `reward_objectives`, and `rewards_summary`; there is no `app/api/routers/v1/` package yet.

The existing routers in `app/api/routers/rewards_summary.py`, `app/api/routers/reward_objectives.py`, and `app/api/routers/reward_notifications.py` instantiate repositories/services directly in dependency helpers and convert domain exceptions into per-endpoint `HTTPException` responses with `detail` strings.

Safe DTOs already exist for the rewards slice in `app/schemas/reward_summary.py`, `app/schemas/reward_objectives.py`, and `app/schemas/reward_notifications.py`, but there is no shared `app/schemas/common.py` or reusable mutation-success/error envelope yet.

Current services and repositories are rewards-only: `app/services/reward_*` and `app/db/repositories/reward_*`; package markers exist in `app/services/__init__.py`, `app/db/repositories/__init__.py`, and `app/schemas/__init__.py`, but no cross-domain conventions are locked in code.

Current API tests in `tests/unit/test_rewards_summary_api.py`, `tests/unit/test_reward_objectives_api.py`, and `tests/unit/test_reward_notifications_api.py` assert route registration and payloads, and they currently expect `404` responses with plain `{"detail": ...}` bodies rather than a standard error shape.
No shared exception handlers, request validation handlers, pagination helpers, or cursor response schemas were found by repo search.
No existing auth, billing, support, dashboard, or Discord browser routers/services are implemented yet, so the foundation spec should stay generic and avoid dragging in unfinished domain behavior.

global_guardrails: Do not expand scope beyond this spec.
Do not add dependencies unless John explicitly approves them.
Do not duplicate Pay commercial business rules in parent.
Do not store sensitive payment details in parent.
Do not build admin dashboards unless explicitly scoped.
Do not redesign stable frontend pages.
Do not make Discord linkage affect rewards or product access unless the spec explicitly says so.
Do not allow frontend APIs to directly award points/rewards/badges.
Do not return raw ORM objects from routers.
Do not mark incomplete items complete.
Append progress entries to the absolute end of `progress/progress.txt`.
Run `python -m compileall app tests alembic` before marking implementation items complete.
Run `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test` before marking implementation items complete.
Keep this foundation limited to shared application-layer primitives and tests; do not start vertical domain implementation in the same run.
Do not modify database models or Alembic migrations unless repo reality proves a tiny support change is absolutely required, and avoid that if at all possible for this spec.

items.title: Create `/api/v1` Router Foundation Package
items.description: Refactor the API registration surface into a reusable versioned router package that preserves the existing `/api/v1` prefix behavior from `app/main.py` while introducing a stable `app/api/routers/v1/` home for current and future domain routers. The implementation should move or wrap the existing rewards routers in a way that does not change their browser paths.
acceptance_criteria: A versioned router package exists under `app/api/routers/v1/` and is the canonical registration point for browser-facing routers.
Existing rewards routes remain reachable at `/api/v1/rewards/...` with no path regression.
The build does not introduce auth, billing, support, or other new domain routers beyond minimal placeholders required to prove the router foundation pattern.
Tests verify the route registration path through `app.main` and the versioned router package.

items.title: Add Common Response Schemas
items.description: Introduce shared schema primitives for standard API envelopes, including a mutation success response and a cursor-pagination response convention that later vertical specs can reuse. Keep the common schemas generic and safe for browser-facing DTO use.
acceptance_criteria: A shared schema module exists for common API responses such as mutation success and cursor/list responses.
The cursor/list response convention supports `limit` and `cursor` style endpoints without forcing page-number pagination.
Shared schemas do not return ORM objects and can be imported by future domain routers without additional dependencies.
Tests cover serialization and the intended JSON contract for the new common response schemas.

items.title: Implement Standard Error Response and Exception Translation
items.description: Replace ad hoc router-level `HTTPException(detail=...)` responses with a shared standard error shape and centralized exception translation pattern for service/domain errors and validation failures. The foundation should be narrow: enough to prove the contract and retrofit the existing rewards slice without inventing broad domain-specific exception taxonomies.
acceptance_criteria: A standard error DTO exists with stable `code`, safe `message`, `details`, and optional `request_id` fields in the documented shape.
Shared exception handling or translation logic is wired into FastAPI so existing routes can return the standard error shape without repeating boilerplate in every endpoint.
At least the current rewards routes use the shared error contract for not-found behavior instead of plain `detail` responses.
Tests cover not-found and validation-path error responses using the standard error JSON structure.

items.title: Lock Service and Repository Boundary Conventions in Code
items.description: Add the lightest code-level foundation needed to make router -> service -> repository dependency direction explicit for future work. This may be package exports, base protocol/type helpers, or narrowly scoped documentation-style tests, but it must avoid overbuilding generic frameworks.
acceptance_criteria: The codebase exposes a clear convention that routers depend on services and services depend on repositories, matching the architecture docs.
The foundation does not add a heavyweight abstraction layer or create unused repositories/services outside the current rewards slice.
Tests or targeted assertions document the intended package boundaries so future build iterations have a locked baseline.
No Pay client logic, auth flows, or dashboard aggregation logic is introduced while establishing these conventions.

items.title: Verify the Application Foundation Baseline
items.description: Run the required verification suite after the foundation changes land and only mark the spec complete once the shared API foundation and its tests pass without reopening unrelated domains.
acceptance_criteria: `python -m compileall app tests alembic` passes.
`docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test` passes.
Foundation-focused tests prove router registration, common schema contracts, and standard error handling behavior.
Only fully completed items are marked complete, and progress is appended at EOF.

completion_definition: Relevant tests are added or updated for router registration, common response schemas, and standard error handling.
No unrelated dirty files are introduced.
No incomplete item is marked complete.
